hypothesis: Videos where the first frame immediately shows the subject clearly will have higher keep rates
method: LLM vision-score first frame for subject clarity (1-10)

hypothesis: First frames that show something unexpected or impossible-looking will drive higher Visual Zeigarnik
method: LLM vision-score first frame for visual surprise (1-10)

hypothesis: Faster cuts in the first 3 seconds correlate with higher keep rate in Shorts audiences
method: Frame diff analysis: count cuts in frames 1-3

hypothesis: Videos showing a human face in the first frame have higher social engagement (Zeigarnik E)
expected_signal: type E frequency higher in face videos
method: LLM vision-score first frame for face presence (y/n)

hypothesis: Videos with a steep retention drop in seconds 3-10 have lower overall retention — early hook retention is the key lever
expected_signal: r > +0.4 between 3-10s retention and total retention
method: Analyze retentionCurve in analysis.json for first 10 data points

hypothesis: Videos with text overlay in first frame have higher click-through and keep rate
method: LLM vision-score first frame for text overlay presence
